About Us

Precision. Integrity.
Excellence.

SPB & Co. is a premier Cost Accounting firm founded by seasoned professionals with a deep commitment to financial accuracy and regulatory excellence. We are registered with the Institute of Cost Accountants of India (ICMAI) and have served over 500 clients across 50+ industries.

Our multidisciplinary team delivers holistic advisory services spanning Cost Audit, GST, Internal Audit, Product Costing, Taxation, and Compliance Management — with the rigor of a Big 4 firm and the personalized care of a boutique practice.

🎯

Cost Audit

Statutory & internal cost audits with precision

📊

GST Advisory

End-to-end GST filing, audit & compliance

🛡️

Taxation

Income tax, TDS compliance & planning

📈

Financial Advisory

Budgeting, forecasting & project reports

Our Philosophy

Why Choose SPB & Co.?

  • ◆ ICMAI Registered Professionals
  • ◆ Pan-India presence across 4 states
  • ◆ Industry-specific compliance expertise
  • ◆ Dedicated client relationship managers
  • ◆ Timely, accurate & confidential delivery
To deliver world-class cost accounting, audit, and advisory services that empower businesses to operate with financial clarity, regulatory compliance, and strategic advantage.

Deep Dive

How We Work

📋
1
Eligibility Check

We assess if your company falls under mandatory Cost Audit as per Companies Act 2013 thresholds.

2
Cost Records Review

Our team reviews cost statements, production records, and CAS-compliant cost books.

3
Audit Report Filing

We prepare and file Form CRA-3 with MCA within the prescribed deadline.

Statutory Cost Audit

Our Cost Audit services ensure full compliance under the Companies (Cost Records and Audit) Rules, 2014. We bring precision and industry expertise to every engagement.

Client Reviews

What Clients Say

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Cost Audit is a systematic examination of cost records and accounts to verify their accuracy. Under the Companies Act 2013, certain companies meeting prescribed thresholds in turnover or net worth are mandated to maintain cost records and get them audited by a Cost Accountant. We assess your eligibility and ensure full compliance.

GST Audit involves examination of GST returns, records, and books of accounts to verify accuracy of declared turnover, taxes paid, refunds claimed and input tax credit availed. Businesses with turnover exceeding prescribed limits may require GST Audit. Our team ensures reconciliation and compliance with minimal risk exposure.

Internal Audit provides an independent, objective assurance to improve operations and add value. It helps identify operational risks, control gaps, compliance failures, and inefficiencies. Our risk-based internal audit approach strengthens your internal controls, improves governance, and helps management make informed decisions.

We prepare comprehensive CMA (Credit Monitoring Arrangement) data including projected financials, cash flow statements, fund flow analysis, and working capital assessments required by banks and financial institutions for sanctioning term loans, cash credit limits, and other credit facilities. Our reports are prepared as per RBI and banking norms.

We provide end-to-end TDS management including applicability assessment, deduction computation, monthly deposit, quarterly return filing (Form 24Q, 26Q, 27Q) and TDS certificate generation. For income tax, we handle tax planning, advance tax computation, ITR filing for corporates and individuals, and represent clients in tax assessments.

Yes, absolutely. SPB & Co. has a pan-India presence with clients and service delivery in Uttar Pradesh, Delhi NCR, Uttarakhand, and Madhya Pradesh. We also serve clients across other states through our digital-first compliance management platform, ensuring timely and seamless service delivery regardless of location.

CMA DataMarch 2026

Privacy Policy

Last Updated: May 2026

SPB & Co. Cost Accountants ("we," "our," or "the Firm") is committed to protecting the privacy and confidentiality of all personal and business information shared with us. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you visit our website www.spbcoca.com or engage our professional services.

1. Information We Collect

We collect information you provide directly to us, including:

  • Contact Information: Name, phone number, email address, and business address when you fill our inquiry form or reach out for consultation.
  • Business Information: Company name, industry, turnover ranges, and nature of service required, necessary to assess your compliance needs.
  • Financial Data: Where you engage our services, we may collect financial statements, tax records, cost records, and other confidential business documents as required for the specific service engagement.
  • Technical Data: IP address, browser type, pages visited, and session duration collected automatically through standard web server logs and analytics tools.
  • Communication Records: Records of your correspondence with us via email, WhatsApp, or phone for service continuity.

2. How We Use Your Information

We use collected information for the following purposes:

  • To respond to your inquiries and provide requested professional services.
  • To prepare cost audit reports, GST filings, tax returns, and other statutory compliance documents.
  • To communicate updates on regulatory changes, compliance deadlines, and service reminders relevant to your engagement.
  • To send you our newsletters, articles, and knowledge updates — you may opt out at any time.
  • To improve our website experience and understand which services generate the most interest.
  • To comply with our own legal and professional obligations under ICMAI regulations and applicable laws.

3. Professional Confidentiality

As practicing Cost Accountants registered with ICMAI (Institute of Cost Accountants of India), all members of our Firm are bound by the Code of Ethics and professional secrecy obligations. All financial data, business information, and documents shared with us in the context of a professional engagement are treated as strictly confidential and are never disclosed to third parties without your explicit consent, except where required by:

  • Court order or judicial / quasi-judicial authority.
  • Regulatory bodies such as ICMAI, MCA, GSTN, or Income Tax authorities as part of lawful compliance filings.
  • Statutory audit requirements under the Companies Act, 2013 or other applicable legislation.

4. Data Sharing

We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal information to any third party for marketing purposes. We may share information with:

  • Service Providers: Cloud storage providers, email service platforms, and IT support engaged under strict confidentiality agreements to assist us in delivering services.
  • Government Portals: GSTN, MCA21, Income Tax e-Filing portal, TRACES — solely for filing returns and reports on your behalf as part of the engagement.
  • Professional Associates: With your consent, where specialized legal or financial expertise is required for complex matters.

5. Data Retention

We retain your data for as long as necessary to fulfill the purpose for which it was collected and to comply with professional, legal, and regulatory requirements. Cost Audit records and financial documents are retained for a minimum of 8 years in accordance with ICMAI guidelines and the Companies Act, 2013. General contact and inquiry records are retained for 3 years or until you request deletion.

6. Data Security

We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your data, including:

  • SSL/TLS encryption for all web-based data transmission.
  • Password-protected and access-controlled file servers for client documents.
  • Role-based access control ensuring only authorized team members handle your files.
  • Regular security reviews and staff training on data protection best practices.

While we take all reasonable precautions, no method of transmission over the internet is 100% secure. In the event of a data breach affecting your information, we will notify you promptly in accordance with applicable law.

7. Cookies & Analytics

Our website uses cookies to enhance user experience and analyze site traffic. We use Google Analytics (anonymized IP) to understand aggregate visitor behavior. You may disable cookies in your browser settings; however, some features of the website may not function properly as a result. We do not use cookies to track your activity across third-party websites.

8. Your Rights

You have the right to:

  • Access the personal information we hold about you.
  • Request correction of inaccurate or outdated information.
  • Request deletion of your data, subject to our legal retention obligations.
  • Withdraw consent for marketing communications at any time.
  • Raise a complaint with the appropriate data protection authority.

To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at office@spbcoca.com or call +91 97167 19137.

9. Third-Party Links

Our website may contain links to external websites including government portals (GSTN, MCA, Income Tax), professional bodies (ICMAI), and informational resources. We are not responsible for the privacy practices of these external sites and encourage you to review their respective privacy policies before submitting any personal information.

10. Children's Privacy

Our services are directed at businesses and adult professionals. We do not knowingly collect personal information from persons under the age of 18. If we become aware that we have inadvertently collected such information, we will delete it promptly.

11. Changes to This Policy

We reserve the right to update this Privacy Policy at any time. The revised policy will be posted on this page with an updated effective date. We encourage you to review this policy periodically. Continued use of our website or services after changes constitutes your acceptance of the updated policy.

12. Contact Us

For any questions, concerns, or requests related to this Privacy Policy, please contact:

SPB & Co. — Cost Accountants
A-221 Rajhans Plaza, Near Aditya Mall, Indirapuram, Ghaziabad, UP – 201014
